import io
import string
import list

fn parse_list(_ s: String) -> List<Int>
  mut result: List<Int> = []
  mut i = 0
  let n = string.len(s)
  mut have = false
  mut cur = 0
  mut neg = false
  while i < n
    match string.byte_at(s, i)
    when Some(c)
      if c == 32 or c == 9 or c == 10 or c == 13
        if have
          if neg
            result = list.push(result, -cur)
          else
            result = list.push(result, cur)
          end
          have = false
          cur = 0
          neg = false
        end
      else if c == 45
        neg = true
      else
        cur = cur * 10 + (c - 48)
        have = true
      end
    when None
      ()
    end
    i = i + 1
  end
  if have
    if neg
      result = list.push(result, -cur)
    else
      result = list.push(result, cur)
    end
  end
  result
end

fn bsearch(_ xs: List<Int>, _ t: Int) -> Option<Int>
  mut lo = 0
  mut hi = xs.len() - 1
  while lo <= hi
    let mid = (lo + hi) / 2
    match xs.get(mid)
    when Some(v)
      if v == t
        return Some(mid)
      else if v < t
        lo = mid + 1
      else
        hi = mid - 1
      end
    when None
      return None
    end
  end
  None
end

fn read_one() -> String
  match io.read_line()
  when Some(s)
    s
  when None
    ""
  end
end

let line1 = read_one()
let line2 = read_one()
let xs = parse_list(line1)

match string.parse_int(string.trim(line2))
when Some(t)
  match bsearch(xs, t)
  when Some(i)
    print("index=#{i}")
  when None
    print("not_found")
  end
when None
  print("not_found")
end
